name: chart-migration-and-toolchain
description: Complete RESTRUCTURE-001c chart migration to unlock the KIND cluster delivery chain; advance HA hardening for quality charts, code-server toolchain init container, and plan ceremony pre-accepted story guard.
sprintGoal: Complete chart migration (RESTRUCTURE-001c) to unlock the KIND bootstrap chain, harden SonarQube+ReportPortal HA (QUALITY-005), wire code-server toolchain init container (DEVEX-007b), and add plan ceremony pre-accepted story guard (CEREMONY-011).

stories.title: Chart migration: platform/charts/ and cluster/kind/charts/ explicit placement
stories.description: Migrate (and verify) all chart directories to the correct tier. Platform-layer charts (_globals, argocd, backstage, chaos-mesh, code-server, crossplane, falco, gitlab, harbor, istio, k6-operator, keycloak, kiali, loki, mailhog, opa-gatekeeper, prometheus-stack, reportportal, selenium-grid, sonarqube, sovereign-pm, tempo, thanos, trivy-operator, wiremock) must be under platform/charts/. Cluster-layer charts (cert-manager, cilium, sealed-secrets) must be under cluster/kind/charts/. The root charts/ directory must be empty to confirm no charts remain in the old location.
acceptanceCriteria: ls platform/charts/ contains all 25 platform charts: _globals argocd backstage chaos-mesh code-server crossplane falco gitlab harbor istio k6-operator keycloak kiali loki mailhog opa-gatekeeper prometheus-stack reportportal selenium-grid sonarqube sovereign-pm tempo thanos trivy-operator wiremock
ls cluster/kind/charts/ contains all 3 cluster charts: cert-manager cilium sealed-secrets
root charts/ is empty: [ -z "$(ls charts/ 2>/dev/null)" ] exits 0
stories.testPlan: ls platform/charts/; ls cluster/kind/charts/; [ -z "$(ls charts/ 2>/dev/null)" ] && echo 'root charts/ empty'; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: AC1 and AC2 describe listing checks ('ls ... contains all 25 platform charts') rather than binary exit-code assertions; a stricter AC would use a for-loop or diff to assert each named chart is present.

stories.title: SonarQube and ReportPortal Helm charts pass HA gate: replicaCount>=2, PDB, podAntiAffinity
stories.description: Harden the SonarQube and ReportPortal Helm charts to meet the platform HA standard. Both charts must have: replicaCount defaulting to 2, a PodDisruptionBudget with minAvailable=1, and podAntiAffinity preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution on hostname. Run the HA gate script against both charts to confirm.
acceptanceCriteria: helm template platform/charts/sonarqube/ | grep -c PodDisruptionBudget returns at least 1
helm template platform/charts/sonarqube/ | grep -c podAntiAffinity returns at least 1
helm template platform/charts/reportportal/ | grep -c PodDisruptionBudget returns at least 1
helm template platform/charts/reportportal/ | grep -c podAntiAffinity returns at least 1
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/sonarqube/; helm lint platform/charts/reportportal/; helm template platform/charts/sonarqube/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; helm template platform/charts/reportportal/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget; show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Specific score 4: named charts and HA fields are clear but the files to modify are not specified. Achievable score 4: two complex charts in one iteration is near the top of the budget. Measurable score 3: no AC verifies replicaCount defaults to 2 in values.yaml; description says 'run the HA gate script' but no AC includes scripts/ha-gate.sh execution against either chart.

stories.returnedReason: AC used exact count assertion ('returns 1') for a multi-component chart that correctly emits multiple PDBs (one per component); AC corrected to 'returns at least 1'

stories.title: code-server chart: add initContainer to Deployment template copying toolchain binaries (kubectl, helm, k9s)
stories.description: Add an initContainers section to platform/charts/code-server/templates/deployment.yaml. The init container image must be constructed in the template source as '{{ .Values.global.imageRegistry }}/{{ .Values.toolchainInit.image }}'. The init container copies kubectl, helm, and k9s binaries into /home/coder/workspace/bin via a shared emptyDir volume. No external registry literals may appear in templates — all image refs must use global.imageRegistry. Depends on DEVEX-007a for the values interface.
acceptanceCriteria: helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -A5 'initContainers' returns at least one init container spec

grep 'global.imageRegistry' platform/charts/code-server/templates/deployment.yaml returns at least one match confirming the init container image is sourced from the platform registry (source file grep, not rendered output)
helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -E 'kubectl|helm|k9s' returns matches showing toolchain binary installation
grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io' platform/charts/code-server/templates/ returns no matches — G6 passes
stories.testPlan: helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ && helm template platform/charts/code-server/ | grep -A10 initContainers; grep 'global.imageRegistry' platform/charts/code-server/templates/deployment.yaml; grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io' platform/charts/code-server/templates/ — show all output verbatim.

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: depends on DEVEX-007a for the values interface; if DEVEX-007a is not yet merged, this story is blocked at the start of implementation.

stories.title: Plan ceremony: warn when >50% of sprint capacity is already-accepted stories
stories.description: Sprint 29 retro identified that 4 of 9 stories were already accepted (passes:true, reviewed:true) when pulled into the sprint. These review-confirmation stories consumed ceremony pipeline execution time without requiring implementation work, leaving 5 unimplemented stories with zero execution capacity. The plan ceremony must check, after assembling a sprint, whether implementation-pending stories (passes:false) are outnumbered or crowded out by review-confirmation stories (passes:true, reviewed:false) or already-accepted stories (passes:true, reviewed:true). If pre-accepted stories make up >50% of the sprint point total, emit a WARNING and suggest removing them from the sprint (they don't need to be re-executed). This prevents sprints from being filled with low-effort confirmations at the expense of new delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -n 'already.accepted\|pre.accepted\|passes.*true.*reviewed.*true\|review.confirmation' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md returns at least one match showing the check is present
stories.testPlan: grep -n 'already.accepted\|review.confirmation' scripts/ralph/ceremonies/plan.md — must return at least one line. python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/ceremonies.py — must exit 0. Show all output verbatim.
stories.returnedReason: Sprint 29: 4 pre-accepted stories consumed execute pipeline, 5 new stories never started; plan ceremony has no guard against this pattern.

smart.notes: All SMART dimensions green. ACs are static-analysis-only (grep + py_compile), no live cluster needed.
